name: ServiceNow, Inc.

profile.description: ServiceNow is the leading enterprise AI platform, orchestrating 80 billion+ enterprise workflows annually across IT, HR, customer service, security, and finance. The Now Platform connects systems, data, and processes with AI to eliminate friction in enterprise operations. Q1 2026 subscription revenue of $3.67B grew 22% YoY, with cRPO of $12.64B (+22.5% YoY). Now Assist AI customers spending $1M+ ACV grew 130%+ YoY. Serves 8,100+ customers including 85% of the Fortune 500.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: ServiceNow has executed one of enterprise software's most successful platform transformations — from ITSM ticketing to enterprise AI platform with $220B+ addressable market [1]. Now Assist AI is the company's fastest-selling product in history, with $1M+ ACV customers growing 130%+ YoY in Q1 2026 [1]. The company shipped 350+ net new AI innovations in its GenAI platform release, and CEO McDermott's $3M personal stock purchase signals maximum conviction in the AI strategy [2]. The Moveworks acquisition adds AI customer service, and Armis (closing H2 2026) expands into cybersecurity. Platform orchestrates 80B+ workflows annually — a massive data moat for AI training.

inspiration.label: STRONG
inspiration.analysis: ServiceNow's mission — 'make the world of work, work better for people' — is specific, validated by product outcomes, and connects to genuine societal benefit of removing enterprise friction [2]. CEO Bill McDermott champions employee-centric leadership, 'inspiring rather than intimidating,' and has cultivated a culture recognized on Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For, Fortune World's Most Admired, and Glassdoor Best Places to Work lists [2]. The company has more than doubled headcount to 29,000+ in five years while maintaining 99% customer retention. McDermott extended his contract to 2030 and purchased $3M in shares during the 2026 software sell-off — powerful insider conviction [2].

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Exceptional financial profile across nearly all metrics. Revenue grew 22% YoY in Q1 2026 with subscription revenue of $3.67B, and management raised full-year guidance by $205M to $15.74-15.78B [1]. Forward P/E of 20.3x is remarkably reasonable for a 22% grower (PEG of 0.99). ROE of 16.1% exceeds target, D/E of just 20.7% is minimal leverage, and free cash flow of $5.1B demonstrates massive cash generation [5]. Revenue 3-year CAGR of 22.4% shows sustained high growth. Only weakness is profit margin at 12.6% (GAAP, due to stock comp) though non-GAAP operating margin is 32%.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: ServiceNow's core thesis is that AI creates more demand for enterprise workflow orchestration, not less. CEO McDermott argues persuasively: 'AI excels at identifying problems and providing advice but cannot execute solutions' — and ServiceNow controls the execution layer [2]. Now Assist AI is the fastest-selling product in company history with $1M+ ACV customers up 130%+ YoY, proving AI is additive revenue rather than competitive threat [1]. The company orchestrates 80B+ enterprise workflows annually — AI agents need this orchestration infrastructure to function. cRPO growth of 22.5% and raised guidance confirm customers are buying more, not less, as AI proliferates. Total RPO of $27.7B (+25% YoY) represents extraordinary forward visibility.

infinite_game.summary: Bill McDermott operates explicitly with long-term platform thinking. His 'Rule of 55+' profitability standard, 2030 contract extension, and $3M personal share purchase during the January 2026 software sell-off reflect genuine infinite game commitment. ServiceNow's Just Cause of removing workplace friction is validated by consistent 22%+ growth, 99% customer retention, and platform expansion from ITSM to enterprise AI over two decades.

just_cause.analysis: ServiceNow's cause — making work work better for people by eliminating enterprise friction — is genuine, service-oriented, and specific [2]. It describes a future state (frictionless enterprise workflows) that doesn't fully exist yet and invites both technology partners and customers to contribute. Validated by 20+ years of execution and 8,100+ customers representing 85% of the Fortune 500.

trusting_teams.analysis: ServiceNow consistently ranks in the top tier for enterprise software on Glassdoor Best Places to Work with CEO approval ratings above 90% [2]. McDermott's philosophy — 'aim for nines and tens, invest in coaching, do not move mediocrity around' — creates high-performance culture. The company doubled headcount to 29,000+ in five years while maintaining culture, and the 'human-AI' narrative frames AI as amplifying employees rather than replacing them.

worthy_rivals.analysis: McDermott publicly acknowledges Salesforce, SAP, and Microsoft as worthy rivals whose integrations push ServiceNow to develop better connectors and expand platform capabilities [2]. The acquisitions of Moveworks (AI customer service) and Armis (security) reflect competitive awareness — rivals' strengths revealing ServiceNow's portfolio gaps that must be filled.

existential_flexibility.analysis: The transformation from ITSM ticketing (2004-2015) to enterprise platform (2015-2020) to enterprise AI platform (2020-present) represents textbook existential flexibility — each pivot built on the previous while expanding TAM 10x [1]. The company didn't defend its ITSM monopoly; it redefined the category. Now the AI agent pivot represents yet another expansion of scope and ambition.

courage_to_lead.analysis: McDermott's $3M personal share purchase during the January 2026 software sector rout — when the stock dropped 10%+ on AI disruption fears — is precisely Sinek's definition of courage: acting in service of the Just Cause at personal financial cost [2]. The 'Rule of 55+' standard (revenue growth + FCF margin > 55) maintains long-term capital discipline even when it constrains short-term growth. Raising full-year guidance by $205M in Q1 reflects confidence in execution [1].

investment_thesis: ServiceNow is the enterprise AI platform that benefits from — rather than is threatened by — the AI revolution. Q1 2026 delivered 22% subscription revenue growth to $3.67B, beating guidance across all metrics, with cRPO of $12.64B (+22.5%) and total RPO of $27.7B (+25%) providing exceptional forward visibility [1]. Now Assist AI customers spending $1M+ ACV grew 130%+ YoY, confirming AI is additive revenue. At a PEG ratio of 0.99 with forward P/E of just 20.3x, the stock is reasonably valued for a 22% grower with $5.1B in annual free cash flow.
thesis_continued: The primary risk is that agentic AI systems could mature to replace enterprise workflow orchestration platforms within 3-5 years, faster than McDermott's 'execution layer' thesis anticipates. However, the Q1 2026 guidance raise of $205M and 99% customer retention provide strong counter-evidence [1]. The trailing P/E of 61x (GAAP, stock-comp distorted) versus forward P/E of 20.3x reflects rapidly improving earnings leverage. McDermott's 2030 contract extension and $3M personal purchase during sector weakness signal long-term conviction rarely seen from enterprise CEOs [2]. ServiceNow's AI governance program and responsible AI framework also position it well for emerging regulatory requirements.
key_catalysts: Now Assist AI scaling beyond 130% YoY $1M+ ACV customer growth, validating AI as a durable revenue accelerator rather than one-time upsell [1]
Armis cybersecurity acquisition closing H2 2026, expanding platform TAM into security operations and adding incremental revenue [1]
Full-year subscription revenue guidance of $15.74-15.78B achieved or exceeded, confirming 22%+ sustainable growth at $15B+ scale [1]
key_risks: Agentic AI systems maturing to replace enterprise workflow orchestration platforms within 2-3 years, faster than the 'execution layer' thesis anticipates
GAAP profit margin of 12.6% reflects heavy stock-based compensation that dilutes shareholders; non-GAAP adjustments may mask true cost structure
Macroeconomic slowdown causing enterprise IT budget cuts and elongated sales cycles, given ServiceNow's premium pricing relative to alternatives
sources: ServiceNow Q1 2026 Financial Results — subscription revenue $3.67B, raised guidance $205M, April 2026

ServiceNow DEF 14A Proxy Statement — AI governance, corporate sustainability oversight, 2026
